title: Today's AI Brief: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab
summary: Exploring AI prototypes from Google's Futures Lab and the future of physical AI interfaces
imageAlt: Image from source article for Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.
imageCaption: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.
imageSourceTitle: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're checking out some fresh developments in AI, straight from the Futures Lab and beyond. I've got a couple of stories that caught my eye, and I'm excited to share them with you. It's always fun to see what's cooking in the world of AI, and today we're going to explore a couple of projects that are making waves.

First up, Google's Futures Lab has been working on some impressive AI prototypes. Students funded by Google have created custom AI-generated stories to help learn Japanese, and an AI tutor that provides real-time feedback on sign language. These aren't just concepts – they're working models that are being tested and refined. I'm particularly intrigued by the language learning project. Who wouldn't want to learn a new language through interactive stories?

Meanwhile, the future of physical AI is shifting towards smarter interfaces. Imagine being able to send commands to a device with just a gesture or a voice command, while keeping your hands free to work. That's where things are headed, according to a recent article in IEEE Spectrum. The example they give is a field technician working on a wind turbine, able to send commands to a diagnostic device without having to put down their wrench. It sounds like science fiction, but apparently, it's not far off.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
